# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.

Prevent race conditions.
# Category: race_condition

#!/bin/bash -x  ## Bash shell shebang to enable execution of scripts (optional)  
export TMPDIR="/tmp"    # Set the temporary directory, default is /tmp    
declare -a data_array       # Declare an array for storing input values.     
dataProcessingFunction() { 
        local tempFile=$(mktemp --dry-run $TMPDIR/myapp-XXXXXX)   # Create a predictable temporary file name in /tmp    
        >&${fd[1]}          # Redirect stdout to the same fd as stderr (file descriptor 2). This is necessary for output redirection.   
        echo "Processing data..." > $tempFile                     # Write some processing information into temp file  
        $@ >>  $tempFile                                     # Execute command line arguments and append result onto a temporary files    
        >&1                                                    # Redirect stderr back to stdout (file descriptor 1). This is necessary for error handling.   
        echo "Done with data processing." > ${fd[2]}                  # Write done message into temp file  
}                                                                     
data_array=($@)                                            # Store command line arguments in array    
for ((i = 0; i <  ${#data_array[*]}; ++i)) {                   # Loop through all the data to be processed.   
        fd=()                                                  # Reset file descriptors for each iteration  
        >&$(( $RANDOM % 2 + 1 ))                                # Randomly select a random number between 0 and 3 (file descriptor numbers are from 4 onwards)    
        ${data_array[i]} | dataProcessingFunction & fd=($$, !!)   # Execute the command line argument asynchronously with file descriptors.   
}                                                                     
wait                                                          # Wait for all background jobs to finish (block until here). This is necessary because we are using '&' in our loop above
